spec_match.rationale: The conceptual diagram and characteristics table accurately describe the privacy and architectural contracts of the Web Speech API.

interactive.rationale: The 'live probe' panel is a generic copy-paste that checks irrelevant capabilities (WebGPU, WebAuthn, CSS.supports) instead of SpeechRecognition, and the toggle button applies an 'is-compared' CSS class that is not defined in the stylesheet.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Matches the parent blurb perfectly, outlining a detailed comparison of cloud vs local data paths.

edge_cases.rationale: Fails to check local speech capabilities or provide a valid capability fallback in the probe code.

references.rationale: Correctly includes the ChromeStatus feature link in the see also list.

design_system.rationale: Applies showcase layout structures, neat borders, and well-separated color-coded badges for risk and safety states.

openQuestions.title: Replace the copy-pasted live probe with a Speech API checker
openQuestions.detail: The current live probe panel checks for GPU, scheduler, and credentials, which are completely unrelated to speech recognition. Rewrite the probe script to test for 'SpeechRecognition' (or 'webkitSpeechRecognition') and query if the 'ondevice' property is present. If present, check navigator.onLine to advise the developer on which path will be used.

openQuestions.title: Remove or implement the comparison state styling
openQuestions.detail: The 'Toggle comparison state' button toggles an 'is-compared' class on the probe panel, but there are no style definitions for it in the page CSS, making the button visually inert. Either implement styling for .live-probe-panel.is-compared or remove the comparison button altogether.
